---
title: "Physical Activity Dashboard"
format:
    dashboard: 
        theme: styles.scss
execute:
    echo: false
---


```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import seaborn as sns
from itables import show

#Load data
physical_activity = pd.read_csv('physical_activity.csv')

physical_activity['status'] = physical_activity['status'].replace({'Adulte': 'Adult', 'Enfant': 'Infant', 'adult': 'Adult'})

# convert columns to numeric(handling 'missing' strings)
cols = ['bmi', 'sedentary_ap_s_day', 'light_ap_s_day', 'mvpa_s_day']
for col in cols:
    physical_activity[col] = pd.to_numeric(physical_activity[col], errors='coerce')

# drop missing values for recommendations to ensure clarity
physical_activity = physical_activity.dropna(subset=['oms_recommendation'])

# convert seconds to minutes/hours
physical_activity['mvpa_min_day'] = physical_activity['mvpa_s_day'] / 60

physical_activity['sedentary_min_day'] = physical_activity['sedentary_ap_s_day'] / 60

physical_activity['light_min_day'] = physical_activity['light_ap_s_day'] / 60

physical_activity['sedentary_hours_day'] = physical_activity['sedentary_ap_s_day'] / 3600

# valueboxes
avg_bmi = physical_activity['bmi'].mean()

avg_sedentary = physical_activity['sedentary_hours_day'].mean()

pct_who = (physical_activity['oms_recommendation'] == 'Yes').mean() * 100
total_n = len(physical_activity)

# Figure 1: Average MVPA
fig_mvpa = physical_activity.groupby(['status', 'sex'])['mvpa_min_day'].mean().reset_index()

fig_mvpa = px.bar(fig_mvpa, x="status", y="mvpa_min_day", color="sex", barmode="group", color_discrete_map={'M': '#2c3e50', 'F': '#e67e22'}, labels={"mvpa_min_day": "Minutes/Day", "status": "Age Group"}, template="simple_white")

# Figure 2: WHO Proportion
who_data = pd.crosstab(physical_activity['status'], physical_activity['oms_recommendation'], normalize='index').reset_index()

who_data = who_data.melt(id_vars='status', var_name='Recommendation', value_name='Percentage')

who_data['Percentage'] = who_data['Percentage'] * 100

who_data = px.bar(who_data, x="status", y="Percentage", color="Recommendation", color_discrete_map={'Yes': '#16a085', 'No': '#d35400'}, labels={"status": "Age Group"}, template="simple_white")


fig_bmi_mvpa = px.scatter(physical_activity, x="bmi", y="mvpa_min_day", color="oms_recommendation",color_discrete_map={'Yes': '#008080', 'No': '#FF8C00'}, labels={"bmi": "BMI (kg/m²)", "mvpa_min_day": "MVPA (min/day)"}, template="simple_white", opacity=0.7)

# Figure 4: Distribution
fig_dist = px.box(physical_activity, x="sex", y="mvpa_min_day", color="sex", labels={"mvpa_min": "Minutes per Day"}, template="simple_white")

```

# Dashboard overview
## Row {height="20%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Average BMI"
dict(icon = "person-arms-up", color = "#7f1d1d", value = f"{avg_bmi:.1f}")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Avg Sedentary Time"
dict(icon = "clock-history", color = "warning", value = f"{avg_sedentary:.1f} hrs")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "WHO Adherence"
dict(icon = "check2-circle", color = "success", value = f"{pct_who:.1f}%")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Total Participants"
dict(icon = "people", color = "#7c3aed", value = total_n)
```

## Row {height=80%}

```{python}
fig_mvpa
```


```{python}
who_data
```

# Correlation
## Row 

```{python}
fig_bmi_mvpa
```

## Row
```{python}
fig_dist
```

# About
This dashboard analyzes physical activity trends. 

```{python}
# Data Source

from itables import show

# Select and rename columns for a cleaner display
table_data = physical_activity[[
    'sex', 'status', 'bmi', 
    'mvpa_min_day', 'sedentary_hours_day', 
    'oms_recommendation'
]].copy()

# Round numeric values for professional look
table_data['bmi'] = table_data['bmi'].round(1)
table_data['mvpa_min_day'] = table_data['mvpa_min_day'].round(1)
table_data['sedentary_hours_day'] = table_data['sedentary_hours_day'].round(1)

show(
    table_data, 
    caption="Physical Activity Participant Data",
    classes="display nowrap compact", 
    buttons=["csv", "excel"],
    # These settings ensure the table fits nicely in the dashboard
    maxBytes=0, 
    autoWidth=True
)
```
